task: Ecosystem maturity research: Node.js, Deno, Bun

package_registry.quality_issue: No vetting process for submission; packages can be low quality, insecure, or malicious

npm_compatibility.notes: npm is the largest developer package registry in the world

production_adoption.description: Node.js remains the safe choice for production applications requiring maximum compatibility and stability

express.details: Long history, stable API, backward compatible; last major version 8 years ago; Express 5 now available with TypeScript support
express.market_share: Still strong in legacy and enterprise projects

fastify.details: Modern, HTTP/2 support, serverless-friendly, robust plugin ecosystem

next.js.details: Built-in analytics, deployment optimization, edge functions, massive community
next.js.capabilities: Handles simple blogs to complex e-commerce platforms

stability_versioning.release_cycle: New semver-major every 6 months (April = even, October = odd)

support_phases.active_lts: 12 months of new features, bug fixes, security updates
support_phases.maintenance: 18 months of critical bug fixes and security patches only

node.js.summary: Production-proven, largest ecosystem, unmatched compatibility and stability. Industry standard with proven operational characteristics.

npm_support.deno_2_0: Fully backwards compatible with Node.js and npm

npm_compatibility.limitations: Some npm packages fail with node16 module resolution; type resolution issues possible

community_size.status: Substantial but significantly smaller than Node.js

production_adoption.positioning: Best choice for security-conscious applications and TypeScript-first projects
production_adoption.enterprise_readiness: LTS channel (v2.1.0+) provides 6 months of support for bug fixes and performance improvements

fresh.status: Most popular web framework for Deno
fresh.features: Zero configuration, TypeScript out-of-box, islands architecture, server-side rendering by default
fresh.deployment: Works great with Deno Deploy for serverless/edge computing

oak.compatibility: Works with Deno, Deno Deploy, Node.js 16.5+, Cloudflare Workers, and Bun

stability_versioning.release_cycle: Minor releases every 12 weeks with bug and security fixes
stability_versioning.lts_channel: v2.1.0+ provides LTS with 6 months support
stability_versioning.lts_end_date: April 30, 2026 - LTS support will be discontinued

deno.summary: Reached production maturity with Deno 2.0. Full npm compatibility removes major adoption barrier. Strong for TypeScript-first, security-conscious projects. Smaller ecosystem than Node.js but growing.

npm_compatibility.native_modules: Supports complex native modules (buffer, fs, path)
npm_compatibility.ecosystem_barrier: npm compatibility argument is dead as of 2026

community_size.market_share: Smaller than Node.js and Deno but growing rapidly

production_adoption.status: Production-ready for many use cases in 2026; test thoroughly in staging
production_adoption.positioning: Performance choice with significant speed improvements for I/O
production_adoption.enterprise_adoption: Following Anthropic acquisition, powering production-grade AI agents and high-concurrency APIs globally
production_adoption.real_world_performance: Teams reduced AWS Lambda execution duration by 35%, lowering costs

elysia.production_use: Used by X, Bank for Agriculture, Cluely, CS.Money, Abacate Pay
elysia.requirement: Requires Bun for production use; optimized for Bun specifically

hono.runtime_support: Cloudflare Workers, Fastly Compute, Deno, Bun, Vercel, Netlify, AWS Lambda, Lambda@Edge, Node.js
hono.flexibility: Works on four runtimes but Cloudflare Workers + Hono is proven production combination

stability_versioning.enterprise_stability: No longer just for side projects - powering production AI agents
stability_versioning.release_timing: v2.0 expected late 2026 to solidify production-ready position
stability_versioning.future_native_support: v2.0 will improve C++ binding support and native module coverage to ~90%
stability_versioning.breaking_changes: v1.3 includes breaking changes (e.g., Bun.serve() WebSocket types reworked)

bun.summary: Rapidly maturing from experiment to production-ready. 98% npm compatible. Superior performance (20-40x npm faster). Backed by Anthropic. Best for performance-critical applications. Smaller ecosystem than Node.js but growing rapidly.

ecosystem_size_ranking.market_maturity: Emerging - 6 years, major adoption with v2.0 (2024)
Early - 5 years, accelerating since Anthropic acquisition

production_readiness.node.js: Gold standard - proven at massive scale
production_readiness.deno: Production-ready as of v2.0 with proper testing
production_readiness.bun: Production-ready with performance advantages; requires careful staging

risk_assessment.deno: Moderate risk; smaller ecosystem but growing; npm compatibility mitigates
risk_assessment.bun: Moderate risk; performance leader but ecosystem still consolidating; Anthropic backing reduces risk

key_insights.npm_ecosystem: Node.js dominates with 3.1M packages and zero ecosystem fragmentation risk. Deno and Bun both access npm ecosystem fully (95-98% compat), eliminating traditional barrier.
key_insights.framework_landscape: Express remains entrenched in enterprise/legacy; Fastify leads greenfield Node.js projects; Next.js dominates full-stack React. Deno has Fresh; Bun has Elysia. Hono provides cross-runtime option.
key_insights.community_maturity: Node.js: Largest, most proven. Deno: 103k stars, ~4% share, growing. Bun: Smaller community but backed by Anthropic with millions of downloads.
key_insights.production_adoption: Node.js proven at Netflix/PayPal/Uber scale. Deno ready post-v2.0. Bun powering AI agents and APIs; cost savings quantified (35% Lambda duration reduction).
key_insights.stability_concerns: Node.js: Mature versioning (6mo cycles, 30mo LTS). Deno: 12-week cycles, 6mo LTS, but LTS ending April 2026. Bun: Rapid iteration, v2.0 expected late 2026, approaching stability.
key_insights.ecosystem_fragmentation_risk: OpenJS Foundation reports community concern about npm security/performance gaps. New registries emerging. Deno's JSR registry offers TypeScript-native alternative.

recommendations_by_use_case.enterprise_legacy_systems: Node.js - unmatched ecosystem, proven stability, maximum vendor support
recommendations_by_use_case.greenfield_high_performance_api: Bun with Elysia or Hono - 20-40x npm performance, 98% npm compat, cost-effective
recommendations_by_use_case.security_first_typescript_native: Deno with Fresh - permission model, TypeScript built-in, modern DX
recommendations_by_use_case.full_stack_web_application: Node.js + Next.js or Fastify + React - most tooling, community, and templates
recommendations_by_use_case.edge_computing_serverless: Deno Deploy + Fresh or Hono on Cloudflare - native edge support
recommendations_by_use_case.microservices_high_throughput: Bun or Fastify - performance-first, Bun offers 35% Lambda cost reduction
